message:
refactor(core): migrate IPC and mux_client to runtime_compat Unix socket layer

Replace direct tokio::net::UnixStream/UnixListener usage in the IPC
server/client and vendored mux client with the runtime_compat::unix
abstraction layer. This consolidates all Unix socket operations behind
a single compatibility shim, preparing for eventual runtime-agnostic
socket handling.

ipc.rs (+28, -29):
  - Replace tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} and
    tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream} imports with
    crate::runtime_compat::unix::{compat_unix, AsyncWriteExt,
    UnixListener, UnixStream}
  - IpcServer::start_listening: UnixListener::bind → compat_unix::bind
  - handle_client_with_context: replace manual BufReader + read_line
    with compat_unix::lines(compat_unix::buffered(reader)) +
    compat_unix::next_line(), converting EOF from empty-string check
    to Option::None pattern
  - IpcClient::send_request: UnixStream::connect → compat_unix::connect,
    same BufReader → buffered/lines migration for response reading,
    with explicit error for server-closed-connection case
  - Test helpers (ipc_handles_invalid_json_request,
    ipc_rejects_oversized_messages): same import and API migration

vendored/mux_client.rs (+3, -5):
  - Replace tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} and
    tokio::net::UnixStream imports with runtime_compat::unix equivalents
  - DirectMuxClient::connect: UnixStream::connect → compat_unix::connect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
